source.sourcePath: raw/sources/RA/04_제품별 인허가 진행 문서/06_HnX-P1, HnX-PB/공통 - CSDT/[Attachment] 4. Summary of Design Verification/1.4. Safety EMC Test report/3.2 [HnX-P1, PB] RF Test report_300.328 Test report.pdf

sourceSummary.oneLine: CE RF test report for HnX-P1 Portable X-Ray System and variant model HnX-PB against EN 300 328 V2.2.2.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Supports design verification/regulatory evidence for EN 300 328 radio testing, including transmitter unwanted emissions and receiver spurious emissions results.
keyPoints: Product item is Portable X-Ray System with model HnX-P1 and variant model HnX-PB.
Test method used is EN 300 328 V2.2.2 and overall test result is PASS.
Transmitter unwanted emissions in the spurious domain and receiver spurious emissions are reported as Pass.
Variant model HnX-PB is described as appearance-design-only change compared with HnX-P1, with interface, exterior material, internal structure, PCB, and software all the same.
The report states it is not related to KOLAS accreditation despite noting KES Co., Ltd. is accredited by KOLAS.

evidence.evidenceText: Report No. : KES-RF240917-R1

evidence.claim: The tested product item is a Portable X-Ray System.
evidence.evidenceText: Product item : Portable X-Ray System

evidence.claim: Testing was performed from 2025.01.01 to 2025.01.06.
The R1 revision dated 2025.05.19 added the variant model name and variant model information.
evidence.evidenceText: 2025.05.19 KES-RF240917-R1 Add variant model name (page.1,4) Add Information about variant model (page.5)

evidence.claim: HnX-PB differs from HnX-P1 only by appearance design according to the report.
evidence.evidenceText: The variant model(HnX-PB) is only changed the appearance design compared to the existing model HnX-P1, and the interface, exterior material, internal structure, PCB, software, etc. are all the same.

evidence.claim: The equipment operates over 2 402 MHz to 2 480 MHz in LE 1 Mbps mode with 40 channels.
evidence.evidenceText: Frequency range 2 402 ㎒ ~ 2 480 ㎒ (LE 1 Mbps); Number of channels 2 402 ㎒ ~ 2 480 ㎒ : 40 ch

evidence.claim: The modulation technique is GFSK.
evidence.evidenceText: Modulation technique GFSK

evidence.claim: The antenna is a chip antenna with peak gain of 3.5 dBi.
evidence.evidenceText: Antenna specification Chip Antenna // Peak gain: 3.5 ㏈i

evidence.claim: The power source is DC 29.6 V battery for operating and AC 230 V charging through an AC/DC adapter output DC 5 V.
evidence.evidenceText: Power source Operating : DC 29.6 V (Battery) Charging : AC 230 V (AC/DC Adapter output DC 5 V)

evidence.claim: Transmitter unwanted emissions in the spurious domain passed EN 300 328 clause 4.3.2.9.
evidence.evidenceText: EN 300 328 4.3.2.9 Transmitter unwanted emissions in the spurious domain Pass
evidence.pageTarget: Transmitter unwanted emissions in the spurious domain

evidence.claim: Receiver spurious emissions passed EN 300 328 clause 4.3.2.10.
evidence.evidenceText: EN 300 328 4.3.2.10 Receiver spurious emissions Pass

evidence.claim: Several EN 300 328 test items were not tested because the device uses a certified module and the report refers to module report ICRT-TR-E210586-0A.
evidence.evidenceText: It is equipped with an certified module and please refer to the module report for test results. Module report No. : ICRT-TR-E210586-0A

evidence.claim: The device does not support geo-location capability.
evidence.evidenceText: This device has not support geo-location capability.

evidence.claim: The report states that it is not related to KOLAS accreditation.

evidence.claim: KES Co., Ltd. states report authenticity can be verified on its website.
evidence.evidenceText: The authenticity of this test report can be found on the verification page of our website (www.kes.co.kr).

reviewFlags.reason: Authority/jurisdiction is ambiguous: the source labels the document as a CE test report and uses EN 300 328, but does not name a notified body or regulatory authority.
The report states KES Co., Ltd. is accredited by KOLAS but also states the test report is not related to KOLAS accreditation.

OCR/text quality has minor issues, including special-character substitutions for units and checkboxes, misspellings such as 'Adress', and an apparent receiver emissions value '266.835 V 100 63.6' that conflicts with surrounding negative dBm values.
